description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the docker:stable image with stages build, cleanup, publish, and deploy, and a before_script that exports IMAGE_TAG from the branch name and date, runs uname -a, and shows docker version. In the build stage add a build:master job that runs only on the master branch and executes docker build -f Dockerfile --pull --no-cache -t "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}/unifi:x86_64-$IMAGE_TAG" .; also add a build:test job that runs on all non‑master branches and runs docker build -f Dockerfile -t "huygens/test/unifi:$IMAGE_TAG" . . In the cleanup stage add a clean:test job that runs on non‑master branches, is manual, and runs docker rmi "huygens/test/unifi:$IMAGE_TAG" . In the deploy stage add a deploy_example job that runs only on master and runs apk add --no-cache py2-pip, pip install docker-compose, docker login -u "gitlab-ci-token" -p "$CI_JOB_TOKEN" $CI_REGISTRY, docker pull "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}/unifi:x86_64-$IMAGE_TAG", docker pull "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}/unifi:latest", and IMAGE_TAG=x86_64-$IMAGE_TAG docker-compose -f docker-compose.yml up -d . In the publish stage add a push job that runs only on master, tags the built image as latest with docker tag "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}/unifi:x86_64-$IMAGE_TAG" "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}/unifi:latest", logs in to the registry, and pushes both the versioned and latest images.
